status: Fixed in fix(taskflow): surface pending close-owned dirty files
finding: taskflow pre-close could report no preflight blockers while close --write was blocked by another readiness issue, leaving close-owned advisory dirty files in the shared worktree. Those files then appeared as foreign dirty work and blocked another lane's next --claim with ATM_TASK_SCOPE_EXPANSION_REQUIRED.
expectedBehavior: When taskflow close owns pending dirty files and close --write is not ready, pre-close should fail closed with an explicit writeReadinessHint blocker, list the pending files, and warn the agent before it leaves or claims another lane.
evidenceOrRepro: On 2026-07-16, TASK-CODEX-0204 left governance-optimization/tasks/TASK-CODEX-0204-shared-worktree-dirty-guard.task.md dirty after closeback was blocked by runner stale / runner-sync readiness. TASK-RFT-0094 next --claim then blocked on that foreign planning amendment with ATM_TASK_SCOPE_EXPANSION_REQUIRED. The file was later committed in 0d85609ec to unblock RFT.
followUp: Add a taskflow pre-close blocker for close-owned dirty files whenever write readiness is already blocked, and make the pre-close envelope return not-ok so agents cannot miss the handoff/commit obligation.
